title: Fish Curry
time: Preparation Time: 15 minutes | Cooking Time: 25 minutes

For the fish marinade: 500 g (1.1 lb) firm white fish fillets (kingfish, cod, or tilapia), cut into 4 cm pieces
½ tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color, mild heat)
For the curry base: 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (adjust for heat)
¼ tsp fenugreek seeds (optional, for depth)
1–2 fresh green chilies, slit lengthwise (optional, for heat)
200 ml coconut milk (full-fat) or 150 ml coconut cream + 50 ml water

Method.title: 1. Marinate the fish
steps: In a bowl, combine fish pieces with turmeric, chili powder, salt, and lemon juice.
Set aside for at least 10 minutes while you prepare the curry base.

Method.title: 2. Prepare the curry base
steps: Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pan until it just starts to smoke (to remove raw pungency), then reduce heat to medium.
Add fenugreek seeds; sauté 10–15 seconds until fragrant.
Add onions; cook slowly until golden brown (8–10 min) for sweetness and depth.
Add ginger and garlic paste; fry 1 min until raw aroma disappears.
Add tomatoes; cook until they break down and the oil begins to separate from the masala (4–5 min).

Method.title: 3. Spice infusion
steps: Stir in turmeric, chili powder, coriander, and cumin. Cook the spices for 30–40 seconds to bloom their flavors.

Method.title: 4. Form the curry
steps: Pour in coconut milk and fish stock. Stir and bring to a gentle simmer.
Season with salt; adjust consistency with water if needed. Simmer 4–5 min for flavors to meld.

Method.title: 5. Cook the fish
steps: Gently slide marinated fish pieces into the simmering curry.
Cook on low heat 5–6 minutes, turning fish only once, until just cooked through and tender.

Method.title: 6. Finish
steps: Turn off heat, stir in lemon juice, and garnish with chopped coriander.
Rest for 2–3 minutes before serving to let the flavors settle.

Chef Tips: Oil separation is a key marker in Indian curries—cook the base until you see oil rising to the top; it means the masala is ready.
Use Kashmiri chili powder for vibrant color without excessive heat.
Do not overcook fish—it should be flaky but moist.
For extra depth, briefly pan-sear the marinated fish before adding to the curry.
Serving Suggestion: Serve hot with steamed basmati rice, jeera rice, or soft Indian breads like naan or paratha.
